objective: Align campaign email.send.requested handoff with the shared Kafka send contract
rollbackNotes: Preserve unrelated dirty changes; revert only this checkpoint scope if explicitly approved.
nextAction: Continue with next highest-priority READY item: egress-evidence-validator-hardening.
implementationSummary: Campaign send handoff now creates rendered content references unconditionally before publishing email.send.requested events.
The legacy content-reference-enabled property now defaults true and is deprecated in code because the Kafka and delivery contracts require a reference.
Focused campaign tests cover configured-off, configured-on, reference-only, oversized payload, and reference creation failure behavior.
Shared Kafka contract tests now allow reference-only payloads and reject inline-only or blank-reference email.send.requested payloads.
residualRisks: High-volume readiness still needs target-like proof for per-recipient content snapshot writes, delivery reference fetches, queue lag, and content-service/database capacity.
Inline rendered content remains enabled by default as fallback data; production high-volume runs should review whether reference-only mode is required.
Content-service rendered snapshot endpoints trust internal callers, so any future non-render caller must preserve sanitization guarantees.
